MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar this day having been assigned by Department 206 for Prove-Up Hearing as to defendant Water Steamship Corporation.
Dept. 514. Judge: Murlene J. Randle. Clerk: Lyssette Bareng. Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 10:25 a.m.

Jason Rose, Esq. appeared for plaintiff Donald Clary.
Order waiving Mr. Clary's appearance at the default prove-up hearing filed August 19, 2021
Mr. Rose appeared via ZOOM for this morning's hearing.
No appearance by defendant Water Steamship Corporation.

Plaintiff's (ASBESTOS) First Amended Complaint for Personal Injury filed November 26, 2019.
Personal Service on defendant Water Steamship Corporation, December 19, 2019.
Default as to defendant Water Steamship Corporation entered on June 15, 2020

Economic Damages - $67,250.00.
Non Ecomonic Damages - $3,067,250.00.
Total - $3,134,500.00.

Proposed Judgment was submitted to and signed by the Court.

Proceedings conclude at 11:07 a.m.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF
Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). Defendant Chiquita Brands International, Inc. withdrew its opposition. The trial date is September 28, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 2, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 24, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 11, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 25, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 3, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
